grammar.title: Section A: Grammar & Vocabulary
grammar.description: 30 multiple-choice questions • Choose ONE answer (A, B, C or D)

questions.question: I haven’t seen Mai ___ she moved to Hanoi.
The meeting was postponed ___ the CEO was ill.
Please remember ___ your laptop to the workshop.
My brother is ___ interested in maths than history.
We’ll miss the bus if we don’t leave right ___.
She asked me where the cinema ___.
They ___ dinner when the lights went out.
A new bridge ___ next year.
Neither the teacher nor the students ___ happy about the delay.
This coffee is too hot ___ drink.
She is the woman ___ won the scholarship.
If I ___ enough money, I would buy that laptop.
Let’s go out, ___ ?
The faster you type, ___ mistakes you make.
I’m responsible ___ locking the office.
We arrived ___ the airport just in time.
How ___ people are coming to the workshop?
She prefers reading ___ watching TV.
I’d rather you ___ here at 8 a.m. tomorrow.
A surgeon is a doctor ___ operates on patients.
We are short ___ paper; could you buy some?
My phone battery is flat. Can I use ___ ?
The film was so sad that I couldn’t stop ___.
He drives ___ than his brother.
We have lived here ___ 2021.
I don’t have ___ free time this week.
The book, ___ cover is blue, is mine.
Would you mind ___ the window?
Tom is the ___ student in our class.
He couldn’t fix the computer by ___ .

reading.title: Section B: Reading Comprehension
reading.description: 20 multiple-choice questions • Read passages and choose the best answer

passages.title: Passage 1 – Community Bike Repair Shed
passages.content: Five volunteers in Lakeside opened a small bike-repair shed beside the railway station. Anyone can borrow tools to fix a puncture, oil a chain or adjust brakes. A simple honesty box suggests a one-dollar donation for using the kit, but payment is optional. In its first month, the shed logged 220 users; half were commuters who discovered the service after missing their trains due to flat tyres. Local mechanics rotate each Saturday morning to give advice, and the town council will soon add solar lighting so cyclists can work after dark.

questions.question: Where is the repair shed located?
The suggested donation for tool use is:
How many users visited in the first month?
Why did many commuters discover the shed?
options: Missed trains due to flat tyres

questions.question: Who offers advice on Saturdays?
What improvement is planned next?
The honesty box is described as:
Which service is NOT mentioned?
The phrase 'rotate each Saturday' means mechanics:
The main purpose of the shed is to:

passages.title: Passage 2 – Remote-Work Survey
passages.content: A regional survey of 600 office workers found that 68 percent preferred a hybrid schedule: three days at home and two in the office. Respondents reported saving an average of forty minutes per day on commuting. However, 41 percent said they felt less connected to colleagues, and 29 percent had difficulty separating work and personal time. As a result, several companies now organise monthly in-person social events and have set a mandatory ‘offline hour’ between 6 p.m. and 7 p.m. to discourage after-hours emails. Early data show a ten-percent drop in reported stress levels since these measures were introduced.

questions.question: How many workers were surveyed?
The preferred hybrid schedule is:
Average commuting time saved per day is:
What percentage felt less connected to colleagues?
Companies introduced a mandatory offline hour between:
One new measure to improve connection is:
Reported stress levels dropped by:
Which challenge affected 29 percent of respondents?
The word ‘mandatory’ is closest in meaning to:
The passage mainly discusses:

listening.title: Section C: Listening Comprehension
listening.description: 10 multiple-choice questions • Listen to audio scripts and choose the best answer

scripts.title: Script 1 – Severe-Weather Alert
scripts.content: Good afternoon. A thunderstorm warning is in effect from 3 p.m. until 8 p.m. Wind gusts could reach 70 kilometres an hour, and heavy rain may cause local flooding. All outdoor sports fields will close at 2:30, and tonight’s food-truck market is postponed to next Friday. If you must travel, allow extra time and avoid low-lying roads.

questions.question: When does the storm warning start?
Maximum wind gusts may reach:
What closes at 2:30 p.m.?
The food-truck market is moved to:
Travellers are advised to avoid:

scripts.title: Script 2 – Bookstore Weekend Sale
scripts.content: Hi everyone. Just a reminder that our annual weekend sale starts this Saturday at 9 a.m. All fiction is half-price, and customers who buy three books receive a free reusable tote bag. The children’s storytelling session begins at 11 and repeats at 2. Parking behind the store is limited, so we suggest using Maple Street Garage, which is free on weekends.

questions.question: When does the sale begin?
Discount on fiction books is:
What bonus comes with three books?
Storytelling sessions happen at 11 a.m. and:
Parking behind the store is described as:

writing.title: Section D: Writing
writing.description: 3 writing tasks • Suggested timing: 45–60 minutes total

tasks.title: Task 1 (110 – 130 words)
tasks.instruction: Your apartment block will have noisy roof repairs for two weeks. Write an email to the building manager asking for the exact schedule and suggesting one measure to reduce disruption for residents who work from home.

tasks.title: Task 2 (150 – 180 words)
tasks.instruction: “Teenagers should have part-time jobs while studying.” Do you agree or disagree? Give ONE reason and ONE relevant example to support your view.

tasks.title: Task 3 (160 – 190 words)
tasks.instruction: A bar chart compares monthly online and in-store sales for a clothing brand from January to June. Online sales rise steadily from $20 k to $38 k; in-store sales fall from $50 k to $32 k. Summarise the main trends and suggest ONE reason for the change.
